fact_nuggets.text: Quick Feet (Japanese: Quick Feet) is an Ability introduced in Generation IV.

Description [ edit | edit source ] Games Description MD T D S Boosts the Pokemon's Movement Speed when it has any status problems. B S L MD GtI Unavailable S MD When the Pokemon gets a bad status condition, its Travel Speed goes up! MD RT DX When the Pokemon gets a bad status condition, like poison or paralysis, its Travel Speed goes up for a while.

Contents 1 Effect 1.1 In battle 1.2 Outside of battle 1.2.1 Generation VIII 1.2.2 Generation IX Effect [ edit | edit source ] In battle [ edit | edit source ] While a Pokemon with this Ability has a status condition , its Speed is increased by 50%. Additionally, a Pokemon with this Ability does not lose Speed due to paralysis . Outside of battle [ edit | edit source ] If a Pokemon with Quick Feet is in the first place in the party (even if fainted ), the chance of encountering a wild Pokemon is decreased by 50%. It shares this effect with Stench , White Smoke and Infiltrator (since Generation VIII ). Generation VIII [ edit | edit source ] In Pokemon Sword and Shield only, this Ability instead decreases the chance of hidden encounters by 87.5%. Generation IX [ edit | edit source ] In Pokemon Scarlet and Violet this Ability has no effect outside of battle.

In battle [ edit | edit source ] While a Pokemon with this Ability has a status condition , its Speed is increased by 50%. Additionally, a Pokemon with this Ability does not lose Speed due to paralysis .
Increases Speed to 1.5 with a major status ailment.
